channel.title: Sample on Hazem Profile
channel.description: Recent content in Sample on Hazem Profile

item.title: Ethics Inside AI Recruiting Systems
item.description:
Paragraph

Artificial Intelligence is no longer a futuristic concept. It is already making decisions that affect people’s lives — who gets a loan, who is flagged by police systems, who receives medical attention, and increasingly, who gets a job interview.

Organizations today receive thousands of applications for a single position. To handle this volume, many companies have adopted AI-based hiring systems that automatically screen résumés, rank candidates, and even analyze video interviews. These systems promise efficiency, objectivity, and speed. However, they also raise serious ethical concerns.

item.title: Anonymous… or Just Logged In? The Ethical Dilemma Behind Course Evaluation Surveys
item.description:
1) Ethical Issues in the Survey Design

1.1 The Illusion of Anonymity

The most immediate ethical issue is deceptive anonymity.

Participants are required to log in with identifiable credentials. Even if their names are not directly asked in the survey, the system has the technical capability to link responses to user accounts.

This creates a potential violation of:

  • Informed consent
  • Transparency
  • Respect for persons

If respondents believe their responses are anonymous but they are technically traceable, the survey design becomes ethically misleading. Even if the organization does not intend to track responses, the mere possibility undermines trust.

item.title: Emoji Support
item.description:
Emoji can be enabled in a Hugo project in a number of ways. :zap:

Emoji Support

The emojify function can be called directly in templates or Inline Shortcodes.

To enable emoji globally, set enableEmoji to true in your site’s configuration and then you can type emoji shorthand codes directly in content files; e.g.

The Emoji cheat sheet is a useful reference for emoji shorthand codes.

N.B. The above steps enable Unicode Standard emoji characters and sequences in Hugo, however the rendering of these glyphs depends on the browser and the platform. To style the emoji you can either use a third party emoji font or a font stack; e.g.

item.title: Rich Content
item.description:
Hugo ships with several Embedded Shortcodes for rich content, along with a Privacy Config and a set of Simple Shortcodes that enable static and no-JS versions of various social media embeds.

X Simple Shortcode

{{< tweet user="GoHugoIO" id="1315233626070503424" >}}

Profile is a customizable single-page portfolio theme for Hugo featuring a contact form and blog. Made by @gurusabarishh.https://t.co/KwYcwfdy16

— GoHugo.io (@GoHugoIO) October 11, 2020

Vimeo Simple Shortcode

{{< vimeo 146022717 >}}


Youtube Simple Shortcode

{{< youtube w7Ft2ymGmfc >}}


Theme Custom Shortcodes

These shortcodes are not Hugo built-ins, but are provided by the theme.
